summary: Iter18 — verified all 3 new capabilities (multi-thread conversation history, Apply Cortex's recommendation, conversation_id wiring through chat + SSE). Backend: 8/8 pytest pass against the live preview URL. Frontend: full UI flow validated via Playwright on /dashboard — sidebar mounts left of chat, +New conversation mints uuid + shows empty state, search filters by title, conversation-item click loads thread, OptimizationStatus shows applied state for a previously-applied finding. End-to-end conversation_id round-trip confirmed: POST /chat with conversation_id persists turns under that id, GET /conversations lists it within ~500ms, and SSE /chat/stream echoes conversation_id in the ready event.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/cortex_conversations.py: clean aggregation pipeline. _ifNull → LEGACY_ID buckets pre-multi-thread rows correctly. Title derived from first user message with newline-strip + 60-char truncate is reasonable. _id stripped via projection on the per-thread GET. 404 on empty thread is correct.

routes/cortex_optimization.py: APPLY_ACTIONS map covers all 5 detector kinds (discovery_stall, qualification_bottleneck, deliverability_risk, copy_conversion_gap, onboarding_stall). Idempotency is enforced by checking applied_at *before* dispatch and returning {already_applied:true} — verified twice in tests. Autonomy routing piggy-backs on existing _execute_queue / _execute_launch — good code reuse, no new approval-queue plumbing needed. Minor nit: `from fastapi import HTTPException` is duplicated inside the function body (already imported at module scope is missing); consider hoisting to module-level import for consistency with the rest of the file.

routes/cortex_console.py: ChatPayload.conversation_id is Optional[str] with strip-to-legacy fallback — backwards-compat is preserved. Both user + cortex turns are stamped with conversation_id (lines 264, 273).
routes/cortex_stream.py: SSE handler also writes conversation_id on both persisted rows AND echoes it in the ready event payload (line 151). Verified via real EventSource read in tests.

ConversationHistory.jsx: useEffect depends on refreshKey so post-send bumps cause a re-list (good). handleNew correctly does NOT reload — fresh empty threads only appear once a message lands. fmtDateBucket uses local toDateString() comparison — works in UTC-skew users but date-bucket boundaries near midnight could land in 'Yesterday' depending on viewer TZ; not a blocker for v1.

OptimizationStatus.jsx: applied state correctly short-circuits the button via finding.applied_at on render (verified in screenshots — Applied banner shown). The local `applied` state syncs from the backend response and persists across re-renders within the session.

Minor (nit, non-blocking): cortex_optimization_log retains `_id` projection elsewhere is fine, but the apply endpoint's update_one writes `applied_at` as a datetime object while the GET endpoints (_serialize) iso-format it — confirmed the round-trip is consistent because _serialize handles both.
test_credentials: Authorization: Bearer test_session_1779636592168 + session_token cookie (user_test1779636592168). Source: /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 fresh conversations during testing (TEST_iter18 hi cortex … and TEST_iter18 stream ping …) under unique conversation_ids; both persist in user's cortex_conversations and now appear in their Today bucket. No cleanup needed — TEST_ prefix is searchable for future cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter18 test file = /app/backend/tests/test_cortex_iter18.py. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://social-sync-ai-1.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/test_cortex_iter18.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/cortex_iter18.xml. The apply-happy-path test is opportunistic — it picks the first un-applied finding from /api/cortex/optimization/log. If all findings have already been applied (likely on re-runs because the test stamps applied_at), the test falls through to verifying the already_applied=true branch, which is still a valid idempotency check. To reset, delete user's cortex_optimization_log rows OR clear `applied_at` on a specific row before re-running. SSE test reads raw event-stream lines and stops at the first ready event — runs in ~10s. The user has autonomy_level=2 so apply queues (not launches); bump users.autonomy_level to 3+ to exercise the _execute_launch branch.
rca of the issue: N/A — all spec items pass cleanly.
